metadata.title: Product Experiences
metadata.description: Outcome-based product experiences for the Premium Long-Haul Airline domain, grounded in airline retailing, servicing, operations, and partner connectivity.

definitions.productBrick: A reusable implementation-facing building block used to realize one or more product experiences.
definitions.productCapability: A high-level, outcome-based experience that delivers customer or business value.

metadata.flowModelNote: Each experience now carries only experience-specific flows. Each flow step lists the bricks and external systems used inside that experience.

capabilities.name: Find and Compare the Right Trip
capabilities.description: Help a traveler understand the best offer, itinerary, and premium options before booking.

flows.title: Find and Compare the Right Trip
flows.description: Help a traveler understand the best offer, itinerary, and premium options before booking.

steps.title: Search trip options
keyFacts: This step shapes first relevance and perceived product quality.
Users decide quickly whether the marketplace understands their intent.
painPoints: Low relevance or weak filtering creates early abandonment.
Users lose trust when the result set feels noisy or poorly explained.

dependencies.name: Offer, Pricing & Availability Management
dependencies.description: Builds the governed flight offer layer across schedules, cabins, fares, and inventory availability.
dependencies.how: Uses Offer, Pricing & Availability Management for builds the governed flight offer layer across schedules, cabins, fares, and inventory availability in search trip options.

dependencies.name: Ancillary, Seat & Upgrade Merchandising
dependencies.description: Monetizes seats, bags, lounges, upgrades, and premium extras throughout the booking and servicing journey.
dependencies.how: Uses Ancillary, Seat & Upgrade Merchandising for monetizes seats, bags, lounges, upgrades, and premium extras throughout the booking and servicing journey in search trip options.

steps.title: Compare offers
keyFacts: This step supports user visibility, decision quality, and operational control.
Users need concise signals, not raw system output.
painPoints: Users struggle when the state is hard to interpret or act on.
Decision quality drops when key signals are delayed or buried.

capabilities.name: Manage a Booking After Purchase

flows.title: Manage a Booking After Purchase
flows.description: Help travelers change, service, and complete order-related tasks after booking.

steps.title: Open booking
keyFacts: This step converts intent into a real transaction or commitment.
Trust, clarity, and feedback matter more here than feature breadth.
painPoints: Users hesitate when they do not trust the outcome, price, or handoff.
Small friction at this point creates disproportionate conversion loss.
